comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I audited the existing P-NCG proof and found and fixed two genuine gaps; the rest of the proof is correct and the core projection lemmas check out numerically (Lemma proj (ii),(iv) verified over 200k random box instances, 0 violations).

**Gap 1 (FATAL): the CG arc line search can fail to terminate.**
The algorithm called the SAME backtracking line search `LS(x_k,d_k,...)` for the CG direction and claimed "Lemma pg shows this loop terminates." That termination proof was specific to d=-g_k. Along a *projection arc*, a direction with <g_k,d_k> < 0 (descent in the usual sense, passing the angle test) need NOT be a descent direction, because active bounds clip the outward components of d_k and the surviving tangential part w can have <g_k,w> > 0. Then arc Armijo fails for ALL alpha>0 and the loop never ends.

I constructed and numerically verified an explicit C^1 counterexample: x_k=(0,0), Omega=[0,inf)xR, g_k=(2,5), d_k=(-3,1) (so <g_k,d_k>=-1<0, and the angle test passes with c1=1e-2, c2=5), f(y)=2y1+5y2+5y2^2. Then P(x_k+a d_k)=(0,a), <g_k,arc>=5a>0, f(arc)-f(x_k)=5a+5a^2, which never satisfies <= eta*5a. Backtracking loops forever.

**Fix:** I replaced the CG search with a *capped* CG search LS^cg with at most M backtracks; if no alpha satisfies Armijo within M steps it returns `fail` and the algorithm keeps the projected-gradient point. Termination is then trivial (Lemma cg) and global convergence is unaffected because it rests entirely on the reference projected-gradient step. I added Remark (Why the CG search must be capped) with the counterexample, and Lemma cg.

**Gap 2: the descent (Lipschitz) lemma was applied at trial points possibly outside the Lipschitz domain.** (A2) only assumed Lipschitzness on D ⊇ L_0, but trial points P(x_k - a g_k) lie in Omega, not necessarily in L_0 ⊆ D; the original "all trial points lie in D" paragraph was circular and yielded an acceptance threshold delta/||g_k|| that depends on x_k, breaking the *uniform* step lower bound underline-alpha.

**Fix:** I strengthened (A2) to require Lipschitzness on the explicit bounded open convex set D = {x : dist(x, conv L_0) < G+1}, G = sup_{L_0}||g|| (< infinity by (A1)+continuity). New Lemma Dtrial shows every reference trial point P(x_k - a g_k) with a in [0,1] and its segment to x_k lie in D (since ||P(x_k-a g_k)-x_k|| <= a||g_k|| <= G), so Lemma taylor applies with the uniform constant L and underline-alpha = min{alpha_min, 1, 2gamma(1-eta)/L} is genuinely uniform. The capped CG search needs no Lipschitz estimate, so no D-claim about CG trial points is needed.

I also ran the corrected algorithm end-to-end on random 20-dim bound-constrained quadratics with active bounds: it converges to stationary points (projected gradient ~1e-8) in ~15-20 iterations and never hangs.

The proof now establishes: each iteration terminates; (x_k) stays in L_0 with strictly decreasing f; sum rho(x_k)^2 < infinity hence rho(x_k)->0; and every limit point is first-order stationary for the bound-constrained problem, for any standard beta (FR/PRP/HS/DY/HZ). Memory remains O(n). Resolving.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I reviewed the existing solution.tex in full and verified its correctness; it is a complete, rigorous answer to the issue.

**Summary of the construction.** Algorithm P-NCG (Alg. 1) extends nonlinear CG to min{f(x): l<=x<=u} keeping O(n) storage by:
- searching along the *projection arc* alpha -> P(x_k + alpha d_k) with an Armijo backtracking test (feasibility maintained automatically: P is the O(n) coordinatewise clip);
- using ANY standard CG scalar beta (FR, PRP, HS, DY, HZ) in the recurrence d_{k+1} = -g_{k+1} + beta_{k+1} d_k;
- a projected-gradient (PG) *reference* search that supplies the guaranteed decrease, plus a *capped* CG arc search accepted only when it returns success AND strictly improves on the PG point.

**Global convergence (Thm 1).** Under (A1) bounded level set and (A2) Lipschitz gradient on an enlarged domain D, the method either stops at a stationary point (rho(x_k)=0) or produces an infinite feasible sequence with strict decrease and sum_k rho(x_k)^2 < infinity, hence rho(x_k) -> 0; since rho is continuous, every limit point x* is feasible and first-order stationary, <g(x*), y-x*> >= 0 for all y in Omega. The result is independent of the choice of beta (Corollary).

**Verification performed (numerical sanity checks):**
1. Lemma 1(iv) and the monotonicity of ||P(x-alpha g)-x||/alpha: confirmed over 10^4 random boxes/points.
2. The Lemma-pg acceptance condition (every alpha <= 2(1-eta)/L is Armijo-accepted): confirmed over 2000 random PSD quadratics with exact L.
3. The Remark "why the CG search must be capped" counterexample (arc Armijo fails for all alpha>0 with inner(g,d)=-1<0 on the boundary): confirmed.

**Logical audit of the subtle points** — all hold:
- bar_alpha_k >= alpha_min is preserved by the safeguarded BB update, so the PG search has a uniform step lower bound underline_alpha = min{alpha_min, 1, 2*gamma*(1-eta)/L} > 0.
- The Armijo-implication chain in Lemma pg is valid because r-tilde(alpha) != 0 for alpha in (0,1] (else Lemma 1(iv) forces rho(x_k)=0, contradicting non-stationarity).
- The capped CG search is harmless: terminates in <=M steps, only accepted when strictly improving, so it cannot break the guaranteed PG decrease.

**Edit made.** The only imprecision was an unhandled boundary case in the proof of Lemma 1(iv): when the maximal feasible step alpha_i^dagger = 0 (e.g. x_i = l_i, g_i > 0) or = +infinity (unbounded box). I rewrote that paragraph to define alpha_i^dagger explicitly and to cover the degenerate alpha_i^dagger in {0, +infinity} cases; the conclusion (||phi(alpha)||/alpha nonincreasing, hence (iv)) is unchanged and now airtight.

The proof is complete and correct. Resolving.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I reviewed the full solution and verified its mathematics. Summary of what is established and checked:

**Construction.** A Projected Nonlinear CG (P-NCG) method that (a) searches along the projection arc alpha -> P(x_k + alpha d_k) with Armijo backtracking, (b) uses ANY standard CG scalar beta (FR/PRP/HS/DY/HZ), (c) guarantees decrease via a projected-gradient (PG) reference search, and (d) only accepts the (capped) CG step when it strictly improves. Storage is O(n) (Remark 2), matching CG.

**Key results, all re-derived and numerically checked:**
- Lemma proj(i)-(v): projection variational inequality; key descent ineq <g,P(x-a g)-x> <= -(1/a)||P(x-a g)-x||^2; rho(x)=0 <=> first-order KKT; the bound ||P(x-a g)-x|| >= a*rho(x) for 0<a<=1 via coordinatewise monotonicity of ||phi(alpha)||/alpha; continuity of rho. I numerically verified (ii), (iv), and the monotonicity claim over thousands of random boxes/points (all pass).
- Lemma Dtrial / taylor: enlarged Lipschitz domain D so the descent estimate f(x+r)-f(x) <= <g,r> + (L/2)||r||^2 holds at every PG trial point (a genuine subtlety that the proof handles correctly).
- Lemma pg: PG-arc Armijo search terminates with step >= underline_alpha = min{alpha_min, 1, 2*gamma(1-eta)/L} and yields f(x_k)-f(x^pg) >= eta*underline_alpha*rho(x_k)^2 > 0.
- Remark (cap M): the projection arc can make a direction with <g,d><0 a NON-descent direction (boundary clipping leaves an ascent tangential part), so an uncapped CG backtracking loop need not terminate. The explicit 2D C^1 counterexample (x=(0,0), Omega=[0,inf)xR, g=(2,5), d=(-3,1), f=2y1+5y2+5y2^2) is correct: I confirmed the arc Armijo test fails for all alpha>0. Capping at M removes the hazard at no cost since global convergence rests on the PG search.
- Theorem (global convergence): valid for every beta choice. Telescoping the per-step decrease gives sum rho(x_k)^2 < infinity, hence rho(x_k)->0, and by continuity of rho plus boundedness of L_0 every limit point is feasible and first-order stationary.

**Numerical validation.** I implemented the full algorithm (FR beta, capped M=20) on a bound-constrained quadratic; it converges in 7 iterations to a KKT point (active upper bound has g_i<0, interior coords have g_i=0), rho ~ 1e-13.

I found no gaps: termination, feasibility, the quantitative decrease, independence from beta, and stationarity of all limit points are all rigorously and correctly proven. The proof task is complete. Marking resolved.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
Independent re-audit of the resolved P-NCG proof (issue.json was a stale 'open' snapshot; the live issue is already resolved and the solution in solution.tex already contains all prior fixes). I did not rely on prior comments — I re-derived and numerically re-verified the load-bearing claims from scratch:

1. Lemma proj(ii) (key descent <g,P(x-a g)-x> <= -(1/a)||P(x-a g)-x||^2) and (iv) (||P(x-a g)-x|| >= a*rho(x), 0<a<=1): 0 violations over 200,000 random box/point/step instances (dim 1-5).
2. Lemma pg acceptance threshold (every a <= 2(1-eta)/L is Armijo-accepted along the PG arc): 0 violations over 3,000 random PSD quadratics with exact L.
3. Remark 'why the CG search must be capped': the explicit 2D C^1 counterexample (x=(0,0), Omega=[0,inf)xR, g=(2,5), d=(-3,1), f=2y1+5y2+5y2^2) indeed fails arc Armijo for all sampled a>0 — confirming the projection arc can turn a usual-descent direction into a non-descent direction, hence the cap M is necessary and the original bug was real.
4. Full algorithm end-to-end (FR beta, capped M=20) on a 20-dim bound-constrained quadratic with active bounds: converges to a KKT point (rho ~ 6e-8; zero active-bound complementarity violations; interior |g| ~ 3e-8).

Logical audit confirms: per-step guaranteed decrease f(x_k)-f(x_{k+1}) >= eta*underline_alpha*rho(x_k)^2 with uniform underline_alpha=min{alpha_min,1,2*gamma(1-eta)/L}>0 (preserved by safeguarded BB keeping bar_alpha_k >= alpha_min); telescoping gives sum rho(x_k)^2 < infinity, so rho(x_k)->0; continuity of rho + boundedness of L_0 give that every limit point is feasible and first-order stationary, independent of beta. The enlarged Lipschitz domain D=conv(L_0)+(G+1)-ball makes the descent lemma valid at every PG trial point with a uniform L; the capped CG search needs no Lipschitz estimate. O(n) storage is maintained.

No gaps found; no edit to solution.tex required. The proof is complete and correct. Confirming resolved.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
Independent audit of the current P-NCG proof in solution.tex confirms it is complete and correct. I re-verified the two previously-identified gaps are properly fixed and re-checked the core claims:

1. Lemma (projection inequalities) parts (ii) and (iv): verified numerically over 200,000 random box instances (random finite bounds, random x in Omega, random g, random alpha) with ZERO violations.

2. Gap 1 fix (capped CG search): re-ran the explicit C^1 counterexample x_k=(0,0), Omega=[0,inf)xR, g_k=(2,5), d_k=(-3,1), f(y)=2y1+5y2+5y2^2. Confirmed <g,d>=-1<0 and the angle test passes (c1=1e-2,c2=5), yet arc-Armijo is rejected for every tested alpha in {1,0.5,0.1,0.01,1e-4}; f(arc)-f = 5a+5a^2 vs rhs eta*5a, never satisfied. So an uncapped CG arc search would loop forever; the M-cap with `fail` fallback is necessary and correct, and global convergence rests entirely on the projected-gradient reference step.

3. Gap 2 fix (Lipschitz domain): the strengthened (A2) on D = {x : dist(x, conv L_0) < G+1} together with Lemma Dtrial gives every reference trial point P(x_k - a g_k), a in [0,1], inside D with segment in D, so the descent lemma applies with the UNIFORM constant L. This makes the acceptance threshold alpha <= 2(1-eta)/L independent of x_k, hence the uniform step lower bound underline-alpha = min{alpha_min,1,2 gamma (1-eta)/L} > 0 and the per-step decrease f(x_k)-f(x_{k+1}) >= eta*underline-alpha*rho(x_k)^2 are legitimate. The telescoping sum then yields sum rho(x_k)^2 < infinity, rho(x_k)->0, and (by continuity of rho and Lemma proj (iii)) every limit point is first-order stationary.

The O(n) storage and applicability to any standard beta (FR/PRP/HS/DY/HZ) are preserved. No remaining mathematical gaps found. Confirming the issue as resolved.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Review and verification of the P-NCG proof (issue rm-08401-1).**

I reviewed the existing `solution.tex` in full and verified its key mathematical claims; the proof is complete, rigorous, and correct. Summary of what is established and the checks I ran.

**What the solution proves.** It gives a concrete *Projected Nonlinear Conjugate Gradient (P-NCG)* algorithm (Algorithm 1) for `min{f(x): l<=x<=u}` with O(n) storage (Remark `rem:mem`), built from:
- a *projection-arc* Armijo line search `x_k(alpha)=P(x_k+alpha d_k)` keeping iterates feasible;
- a guaranteed-decrease *projected-gradient reference search* `LS^pg`;
- an *optional capped CG search* `LS^cg` along the standard direction `d_{k+1}=-g_{k+1}+beta_{k+1} d_k` (FR/PRP/HS/DY/HZ), accepted only when it strictly improves.

Global convergence (Theorem 1): either it stops at a stationary point, or it produces an infinite feasible decreasing sequence with `rho(x_k)->0` and every limit point first-order stationary for the box-constrained problem. The result is independent of the CG scalar (Corollary), since the guaranteed decrease comes from the reference search.

**Logical chain (all sound).** Projection optimality (Lemma proj i) -> key descent ineq (ii) -> stationarity equivalence rho=0 iff KKT (iii) -> scaling bound `||P(x-alpha g)-x|| >= alpha*rho(x)` for alpha<=1 (iv) -> continuity of rho (v). The enlarged Lipschitz domain D (eq. Ddef) plus Lemma `Dtrial` make the descent estimate valid at every reference trial point with a step lower bound independent of x_k, giving per-step decrease `f(x_k)-f(x_{k+1}) >= eta*alpha_underline*rho(x_k)^2`. Telescoping + bounded-below f yields the summable series and `rho(x_k)->0`; Bolzano-Weierstrass + continuity of rho finish it.

**Subtle points I checked specifically.**
- Lemma proj(iv): the per-coordinate argument that `|phi_i(alpha)|/alpha` is nonincreasing (constant `|g_i|` on the feasible sub-step, then `<= |g_i|` once pinned at a bound), including mixed-coordinate cases. Verified mono over 5000 random boxes.
- The "first equivalence uses r(alpha)!=0" step in Lemma `pg`: indeed `r(alpha)=0` would force `rho(x_k)=0` by (iv), contradicting non-stationarity. Correct.
- Remark `rem:cgterm`: confirmed the 2D C^1 example (`g=(2,5)`, `d=(-3,1)`, descent `g.d=-1<0`, `Omega=[0,inf)xR`, `f=2y1+5y2+5y2^2`) has projected-arc Armijo failing for ALL alpha>0, which justifies *capping* the CG search and shows why the projection arc, unlike the unconstrained case, can make a "descent" direction non-improving.

**Numerical verification** (`verify.py` in workspace):
- proj (ii), (iv), monotonicity: all True over thousands of random instances.
- rem:cgterm example: Armijo fails for all alpha: True.
- Full P-NCG run on a bound-constrained quadratic with active bounds: converges in 14 iters to `rho ~ 5e-10` and satisfies the componentwise KKT conditions at all active/inactive bounds (KKT_satisfied=True).

**Conclusion.** The proof correctly devises an O(n) projected nonlinear-CG extension and establishes global convergence to a stationary point of the bound-constrained problem under standard assumptions (A1) bounded level set, (A2) Lipschitz gradient, and an Armijo backtracking line search. No gap found; the solution stands as written. (LaTeX compile skipped: no toolchain in the environment, but the document is self-contained and well-formed.)

Marking resolved.
